digest.component_or_area: v1.0 release pipeline and store-compatibility gate
propositions: A v* tag can publish artifacts without machine proof that the tagged SHA is reachable from reviewed-and-landed main, and tags are left unprotected.

There is no committed store-format-version / required-capability record, so a v1.0 binary cannot fail-closed on a store it cannot correctly interpret; this gate must ship in v1.0 itself and cannot be added retroactively.

The compatibility gate must treat an absent record as legacy (version 0, compatible, fail-open) and fail-closed only when the record is present-but-incompatible or corrupt, with ensure-registry writing the record on next covered write to preserve upgrade-in-place and rollback safety.
python -m build bakes COMMIT = None into the published wheel and CI installs the source tree instead of the built artifact, so the null-commit defect is uncaught until after PyPI immutability.
The release pipeline resolves actions, build tools, and a privileged OIDC binary from mutable references, inconsistent with the SHA-pinning convention already used in gerrit-verify.yaml.
Removed env vars and TOML keys affecting store location, write/sync gates, auth, or lifecycle policy are silently ignored rather than failing loud with a targeted migration message and nonzero exit.
